evidence.quote: CUMS program stimulation factors include the following [7]: overnight lighting, fasting, water banning, hot water swimming, and cold water swimming. The above stimulation factors were randomly assigned seven days a week, and two kinds of compound factor stimulation were randomly arranged daily.

There were significant differences in body weight, sugar water preference, and immobility time between the model group, the administration group, and the blank group, indicating that the CUMS model was successful.

[7] T. Xiao, T. Y. Ma, X. Q. Xu, and K. W. Wang, “Establishment and behavior evaluation of rat chronic unpredictable mild stress and mouse social defeat models,” Acta Neuropharmacologica, vol. 8, no. 1, pp. 45–53, 2018.

step_scores.label: fully_supported

evidence.quote: The single-dose subgroup received the drug for one day, and the multiple-dose subgroup received the drug for three months... administration group (who were administered doses at rates of 65mg/kg, 130mg/kg, 260mg/kg, and 520mg/kg)... long-term administration group was also subdivided into... (65mg/kg and 520mg/kg).
After three months of the administration, the body weight and sugar water preference of rats in the administration group were significantly higher than those in the model group.
